title: GDPR Encryption at Rest Audit Evidence

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `aws:cloudtrail` (CreateVolume, CreateDBInstance, PutBucketEncryption), Azure Activity / resource graph exports (`mscs:azure:auditlog`), `tenable:vuln` or agent inventory with encryption flags, `gdpr_data_store_register.csv`
app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060)
spl:
| inputlookup gdpr_data_store_register.csv WHERE processes_personal_data="true"
| lookup cloud_asset_encryption_lookup resource_id OUTPUT encryption_at_rest, last_scan_date, kms_key_arn
| eval scan_age_days=if(isnotnull(last_scan_date), round((now()-strptime(last_scan_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0), 9999)
| eval compliance_status=case(
    encryption_at_rest="true", "COMPLIANT",
    encryption_at_rest="false", "NON_COMPLIANT",
    isnull(encryption_at_rest), "UNKNOWN")
| where compliance_status!="COMPLIANT" OR scan_age_days > 90
| table resource_id, system_name, data_category, encryption_at_rest, compliance_status, scan_age_days, kms_key_arn
| sort compliance_status
value: Article 32(1)(a) requires pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data where appropriate. This use case aggregates technical evidence that storage volumes, databases, object stores, and backup targets that process personal data are encrypted at rest — surfacing misconfigured buckets, unencrypted snapshots, and hosts lacking disk encryption so the DPO can prove the measure is operational, not aspirational.
implementation: (1) Maintain `gdpr_data_store_register.csv` linking cloud resource IDs and on-prem volumes to systems of record for personal data; (2) ingest CloudTrail / Azure audit events that reflect encryption configuration changes; (3) join nightly Tenable or CSPM exports for at-rest encryption posture; (4) alert on `NON_COMPLIANT` or stale `UNKNOWN` assets; (5) retain dashboard exports for Art. 32 accountability evidence packs.
visualization: Table (non-compliant stores), Single value (count lacking encryption), Bar chart (violations by data category), Timeline (encryption configuration changes).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
